message:
chore(node): update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -590,10 +590,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.10.1","title":"Define claim-to-harness mapping and non-deceptive result schema","description":"Define how claims map to executable checks, how harness invocation works, and what structured result schema distinguishes executed checks from report-only planning.","design":"Model both normal execution mode and explicit dry-run mode. The latter is allowed, but only when the output makes it impossible to confuse with a completed verification run.","acceptance_criteria":"- Claim-to-check mapping is explicit.
- Harness protocol and result schema are defined.
- Dry-run semantics are unambiguous.","notes":"Defined the non-deceptive external reproduction contract in docs and checkers: procedure_ref, harness_kind, measurement_key, and explicit plan vs executed / not_run semantics. Targeted validation passed with python3 scripts/check_external_reproduction.py --json and python3 -m unittest tests.test_check_external_reproduction. The broader placeholder inventory scan still fails on the unrelated DGIS anchor rule dgis_placeholder_barrier_context.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"SilverVale","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:26.634750206Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.422728011Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.422692405Z","close_reason":"Defined claim mapping, harness protocol, and non-deceptive plan/executed schema in docs/checkers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","placeholder-remediation","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:31.422962547Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:26.877247575Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","title":"Implement real reproduction harness execution and explicit dry-run mode","description":"Implement the actual harness-backed verification flow and explicit dry-run/report-only mode. The dedicated validation bead will add the broad end-to-end matrix, schema assertions, and operator-facing logging checks.","design":"Normal mode should execute referenced checks; dry-run mode should produce planning/reporting output with unmistakable status markers. This bead should also emit the core status fields and execution hooks needed for downstream validation.","acceptance_criteria":"- Normal mode executes real verification work.
- Dry-run mode is explicit and never marked as pass.
- The implementation exposes stable status/logging hooks for follow-on validation.","notes":"Implemented real procedure execution in scripts/reproduce.py with explicit plan vs executed schema, per-claim execution_state/result_kind, command/duration/exit-code logging, timeout/error handling, and claim registry mapping fields in docs/headline_claims.toml. Added unit coverage in tests/test_reproduce.py and verified with: python3 -m py_compile scripts/reproduce.py tests/test_reproduce.py; python3 -m unittest tests.test_reproduce tests.test_check_external_reproduction; python3 scripts/check_external_reproduction.py --json; python3 scripts/reproduce.py --dry-run --json; python3 scripts/reproduce.py --claim HC-001 --json --timeout-seconds 30; python3 scripts/reproduce.py --claim HC-002 --json --timeout-seconds 30. Agent Mail inbox stayed empty; contact handshakes/messages are still blocked by transient MCP Agent Mail database errors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenLake","owner":"GreenLake","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.182821278Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:37:55.806601843Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:37:55.806574542Z","close_reason":"Implemented harness-backed reproduction execution, explicit dry-run/report schema, and unit coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","placeholder-remediation","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.423645330Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:32.345874843Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","title":"Add reproduce-harness e2e scripts, failure-path coverage, and verbose execution logging","description":"Add the high-signal validation surface for `scripts/reproduce.py`: end-to-end scripts, failure-path coverage, and verbose execution logs that prove the harness actually ran or that the tool was intentionally in dry-run mode.","design":"Tests should cover successful execution, missing harness mapping, harness failure, and dry-run/report-only mode. Logging should capture the claim identifier, resolved harness target, command/result summary, and the explicit status distinction between executed verification and non-executing planning output.","acceptance_criteria":"- There are end-to-end scripts/tests for success, harness failure, missing mapping, and dry-run behavior.
- Output and logs make it impossible to confuse dry-run/reporting with a passing verification run.
- Machine-readable result schema is asserted in tests.
- User-facing docs/examples show how to interpret the detailed logs and statuses.","notes":"This bead protects operator trust in verification claims by making execution evidence inspectable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.543251023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:20.232973660Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.543251023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.543251023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.11","title":"Harden DGIS no-barrier semantics instead of synthetic pass-through receipts","description":"Harden DGIS no-barrier behavior so the system no longer fabricates synthetic pass-through receipts when no matching barrier exists. The audit found `security/dgis/barrier_primitives.rs` building synthetic barriers/receipts with `passthrough-*` IDs in no-match cases.","design":"The system should distinguish clearly between:
- a real barrier check that passed
- a no-barrier / not-applicable condition
- a policy violation caused by missing required barrier coverage

A fabricated pass receipt blurs those categories and undermines auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"- No-barrier semantics are explicit and auditable.
- Synthetic pass-through receipt fabrication is removed or strictly limited to intentional non-live contexts.
- Callers/tests/audit codes reflect the new model.","notes":"This is a lower-confidence finding than the live CLI demo surfaces, but it is exactly the kind of audit shortcut that can erode trust in receipts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.735636861Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.928397068Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dgis","placeholder-remediation","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.978095298Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2fqyv.11","title":"Harden DGIS no-barrier semantics instead of synthetic pass-through receipts","description":"Harden DGIS no-barrier behavior so the system no longer fabricates synthetic pass-through receipts when no matching barrier exists. The audit found `security/dgis/barrier_primitives.rs` building synthetic barriers/receipts with `passthrough-*` IDs in no-match cases.","design":"The system should distinguish clearly between:
- a real barrier check that passed
- a no-barrier / not-applicable condition
- a policy violation caused by missing required barrier coverage

A fabricated pass receipt blurs those categories and undermines auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"- No-barrier semantics are explicit and auditable.
- Synthetic pass-through receipt fabrication is removed or strictly limited to intentional non-live contexts.
- Callers/tests/audit codes reflect the new model.","notes":"This is a lower-confidence finding than the live CLI demo surfaces, but it is exactly the kind of audit shortcut that can erode trust in receipts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.735636861Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:40:05.055926647Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:40:05.055892363Z","close_reason":"Completed: DGIS no-barrier receipts are explicit and auditably validated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dgis","placeholder-remediation","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:27.978095298Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","title":"Define explicit DGIS no-barrier semantics and receipt model","description":"Define the correct semantics for no-barrier cases: explicit NotApplicable / NoBarrier receipt, fail-closed missing-coverage error, or another deliberate model that does not masquerade as a real barrier pass.","design":"Resolve the semantics first, then update event codes, receipt types, and caller expectations accordingly.","acceptance_criteria":"- No-barrier state model is explicit.
- Receipt/event implications are documented.
- Follow-on implementation scope is clear.","notes":"Preserve auditability: downstream readers should always be able to tell what actually happened.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.283697574Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:04.408450797Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:04.408428976Z","close_reason":"Defined DGIS no-barrier contract: authoritative pass receipts for matched barriers, explicit not_applicable receipts for no-match cases, event code DGIS-BARRIER-007, and aligned spec/inventory updates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","dgis","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:31.656442103Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.520568299Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","title":"Implement explicit DGIS no-barrier behavior and caller updates","description":"Implement the chosen DGIS semantics and update barrier callers so no-match cases no longer emit fabricated pass-through receipts. Exhaustive receipt-matrix proof and audit-log assertions are handled in the validation bead.","design":"Update receipt construction, event code selection, and call-site expectations together so the semantics stay coherent. Emit the core receipt/log fields that the validation bead will later ratchet.","acceptance_criteria":"- Synthetic pass-through receipt fabrication is removed from the audited path.
- Callers consume the new semantics correctly.
- Core audit outputs are explicit enough for downstream validation to assert the distinctions.","notes":"Do not leave partial compatibility layers that preserve the old ambiguous behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:28.829338298Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:10.965921308Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:10.965897464Z","close_reason":"Implemented DGIS no-barrier remediation in barrier_primitives.rs: removed synthetic passthrough receipts, emit explicit not_applicable receipts (DGIS-BARRIER-007), bind successful checks to authoritative barrier IDs, and updated core tests/spec/inventory/artifact. Python DGIS gate passed; full Rust cargo check/test remain blocked by unrelated crates/franken-node/src/security/ssrf_policy.rs missing test_template().","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dgis","implementation","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.068300680Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:32.582489070Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","title":"Add DGIS receipt matrix tests and audit-log coverage for no-barrier semantics","description":"Add the validation matrix for DGIS no-barrier semantics so receipt behavior, caller handling, and audit logs stay precise over time. This bead should prove that no-match cases are no longer disguised as ordinary passes.","design":"Use targeted unit tests for receipt construction, integration tests for caller behavior, and audit-log assertions that differentiate pass, not-applicable/no-barrier, and missing-required-barrier outcomes. Logs and receipts should expose enough detail for downstream audit tooling to classify the event without heuristics.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit and integration tests cover pass, no-barrier/not-applicable, and missing-coverage cases.
- Receipts and audit logs encode the distinctions explicitly.
- No fabricated pass-through receipts survive on the audited path.
- Docs/specs/tests explain the new receipt matrix clearly enough for future maintainers.","notes":"This is where the branch becomes auditably correct instead of merely less suspicious.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:20.600079951Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","title":"Add DGIS receipt matrix tests and audit-log coverage for no-barrier semantics","description":"Add the validation matrix for DGIS no-barrier semantics so receipt behavior, caller handling, and audit logs stay precise over time. This bead should prove that no-match cases are no longer disguised as ordinary passes.","design":"Use targeted unit tests for receipt construction, integration tests for caller behavior, and audit-log assertions that differentiate pass, not-applicable/no-barrier, and missing-required-barrier outcomes. Logs and receipts should expose enough detail for downstream audit tooling to classify the event without heuristics.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit and integration tests cover pass, no-barrier/not-applicable, and missing-coverage cases.
- Receipts and audit logs encode the distinctions explicitly.
- No fabricated pass-through receipts survive on the audited path.
- Docs/specs/tests explain the new receipt matrix clearly enough for future maintainers.","notes":"This is where the branch becomes auditably correct instead of merely less suspicious.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:39:58.143739778Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:39:58.143716966Z","close_reason":"Completed: DGIS receipt matrix and audit-log coverage added and verified","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.901149145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.12","title":"Run the final placeholder-remediation ratchet and close the audit","description":"Close the remediation program with a final audit and ratchet. This branch exists so the effort ends in a clean, provable steady state rather than a pile of partially fixed branches with lingering temporary exceptions.","design":"Use the inventory/scanner work plus the completed remediation branches to:
- remove temporary allowlist entries that are no longer needed
- prove live surfaces are clean
- update docs/specs/contracts so the repository tells the truth about itself","acceptance_criteria":"- Final audit passes with the intended steady-state allowlist.
- No audited live-surface placeholder remains unresolved.
- Docs/specs/contracts summarize the new truthfully-implemented state.","notes":"This branch is the exit criterion for the whole program.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.375630906Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.928849521Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["governance","placeholder-remediation","ratchet"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.612354637Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","title":"Execute final repo-wide audit and ratchet down placeholder allowlists","description":"Execute the final repo-wide audit, shrink or eliminate temporary allowlist entries, and prove that live surfaces no longer depend on demo/sample/placeholder behavior. This bead should only land after each remediation branch has its validation/logging endpoint in place.","design":"Run the scanner against the post-remediation repo, review any residual hits, and explicitly justify any remaining intentional simulations. Re-run the branch-level unit/integration/e2e proof points and capture the resulting audit evidence so the end state is mechanically checkable and human-reviewable.","acceptance_criteria":"- Final scanner run is clean for live surfaces.
- Temporary allowlist entries are removed or minimized with explicit rationale.
- Residual intentional simulations are only in accepted non-live buckets.
- Branch-level validation artifacts/logs exist for every remediated placeholder family.
- The final audit summary references the exact beads, test suites, and logging evidence that justify closure.","notes":"This bead should reference every completed branch before closure and should fail rather than hand-wave any unresolved ambiguity.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:29.915243157Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:20.960804551Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","placeholder-remediation","ratchet"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:40.203252736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:41.649772258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:42.005812448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:30.151791671Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:32.811322409Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:33.046006830Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:33.274510845Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:40.573757910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:33.735696118Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:33.963522420Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:40.930772595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:41.287868342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.12.2","title":"Update durable docs/specs/contracts to encode the new steady state","description":"Update the durable project contract after the code work is done: docs, specs, tests, and bead notes should explain the new reality so future contributors do not rebuild the placeholder behaviors by accident.","design":"Capture the new steady state in the same places the old misleading state was encoded: README/AGENTS guidance when relevant, specs, artifacts, and any operator-facing command docs.","acceptance_criteria":"- The repository contract matches the implemented steady state.
- Future contributors can understand the new rules without re-reading the original audit.
- Program bead closure notes are enough to explain what changed and why.","notes":"Treat this as the documentation counterpart to the final audit ratchet.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:30.460276118Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:35.383554499Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","docs","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:30.700380699Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.12.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:35.383508704Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -615,19 +615,19 @@
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.5.3","title":"Replace fake API performance baselines with measured or explicit semantics","description":"Replace hard-coded zero performance baselines and stale skeleton-oriented contract artifacts with truthful measured outputs or explicit non-availability semantics.","design":"Perf baselines should come from measured runs or be intentionally omitted/flagged unavailable. Update artifacts, docs, and tests so they no longer encode fake `0.0` latency baselines.","acceptance_criteria":"- Endpoint report baselines are measured or explicitly unavailable.
- No product contract treats zero placeholders as real performance data.
- Relevant docs/gates/tests are updated.","notes":"This bead closes the observability honesty gap after the service-boundary work lands.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:39.802687149Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:46.416782412Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","contracts","placeholder-remediation","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:40.073908505Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:46.416737839Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.5.4","title":"Add control-plane contract/e2e coverage and transport/perf provenance logging","description":"Add the validation layer for the control-plane service branch: unit coverage, integration/CLI/e2e checks for the chosen service semantics, and structured logs that explain request lifecycle and performance-baseline provenance.","design":"Cover the truthful steady state end-to-end. If the service is real, tests should exercise request routing, cancellation/lifecycle, and measured perf reporting. If the surface is explicitly quarantined, tests should prove the non-availability semantics and ensure operator-facing output cannot be confused with a live service. In both cases, logs should explain how latency baselines were measured or why they are unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit and integration tests cover the chosen service-boundary semantics.
- CLI/e2e or contract-level checks prove that operators see truthful availability/performance behavior.
- Structured logs or artifacts capture request lifecycle events and perf-baseline provenance.
- Docs/specs/gates are updated to reference the new test and logging workflow.","notes":"This bead makes the branch mechanically trustworthy for users instead of merely less misleading in the code.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.407815417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:19.108081938Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.407815417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.407815417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.407815417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.6","title":"Replace validation-only schema migration execution with real state transforms","description":"Upgrade schema migration execution from validation-only scaffolding to a real state-transforming engine. The audit showed that `connector/schema_migration.rs::execute_plan()` only validates step chaining and then returns `Applied`, without mutating connector state.","design":"This branch should define an executable migration-step model, real transformation semantics, idempotency guarantees, rollback/error behavior, and receipt/reporting detail that correspond to real state changes rather than simulated success.","acceptance_criteria":"- Migration execution mutates real state according to plan steps.
- Receipts capture actual applied work and failure detail.
- Idempotency/rollback/conformance behavior is covered by tests.","notes":"Because backwards compatibility is not a constraint here, prefer the cleanest executable model over preserving the current scaffolding shape.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:40.416218393Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.926139731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector","migration","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:40.688225122Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.6.1","title":"Define executable migration-step semantics and receipt contract","description":"Define the migration-step execution model: how hints become concrete mutations, what state shape is transformed, how receipts describe per-step outcomes, and what rollback/idempotency guarantees exist.","design":"Model the migration engine as an actual state machine rather than a chain validator. Spell out failure semantics, partial-application handling, and how deterministic replay/testing should work.","acceptance_criteria":"- Executable migration semantics are explicit.
- State mutation, failure, and rollback rules are documented.
- Receipt fields required for real execution are identified.","notes":"This bead should make the implementation bead mostly mechanical.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:41.032864010Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.615217628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","migration","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.615134713Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:41.306999977Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Spell out failure semantics, partial-application handling, and how deterministic replay/testing should work.","acceptance_criteria":"- Executable migration semantics are explicit.
- State mutation, failure, and rollback rules are documented.
- Receipt fields required for real execution are identified.","notes":"This bead should make the implementation bead mostly mechanical.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:41.032864010Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-05T23:31:26.143920078Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","migration","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.615134713Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:41.306999977Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.6.2","title":"Implement the real state-transforming schema migration executor","description":"Implement the real state-transforming migration executor and retire the current chain-validation-only success path.","design":"Apply the model bead directly: execute mutations, accumulate actual step outcomes, and ensure failure leaves the system in a defined state rather than just reporting a simulated result.","acceptance_criteria":"- `execute_plan()` or its replacement performs real transformations.
- Success/failure receipts reflect actual work.
- Validation-only scaffolding path is gone from production behavior.","notes":"Keep the API narrow and explicit so future connectors can share the execution engine instead of re-implementing ad hoc transforms.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:41.656869470Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:46.683028738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","migration","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:41.936779847Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:46.682965831Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.6.3","title":"Add migration conformance/e2e coverage for real state mutation","description":"Add the conformance, idempotency, rollback, and e2e coverage that proves migration execution is now real rather than simulated.","design":"Use realistic fixture states, multi-step chains, partial failure cases, and re-application checks. Cover both happy path and operator-visible failure behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit and integration coverage exercises actual state mutation.
- Idempotency and rollback invariants are asserted.
- CLI/e2e coverage reflects real migration effects.
- Test logs/artifacts make mutation steps and failure points inspectable.","notes":"This is the ratchet that prevents regression back to validation-only success receipts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:42.285814605Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:23.970601526Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["migration","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:42.569429535Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:46.948075379Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.7","title":"Replace simulated fuzz gate with harness-backed execution","description":"Replace the simulated fuzz corpus gate with a harness-backed runner that actually executes targets and reports real crash/hang/coverage outcomes. The audit showed that `connector/fuzz_corpus.rs::run_gate()` currently treats any seed containing the string `crash` as a crash and hard-codes hangs and coverage.","design":"The production path should execute a real adapter/harness contract. Deterministic simulations may still exist, but only as explicit test adapters. Real reports must carry real target execution results, reproducer info, hang accounting, and coverage semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"- Production fuzz gate no longer uses string-trigger simulation.
- Crash/hang/coverage data come from real execution or explicit harness output.
- Deterministic test adapters remain available without contaminating the live path.","notes":"Keep the contract flexible enough to support multiple fuzz engines/targets without hard-coding a single harness forever.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:42.923375515Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.926606090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","placeholder-remediation","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:43.202801609Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.7.1","title":"Define fuzz target adapter and real execution/reporting contract","description":"Define the target adapter and execution contract for the fuzz gate: target discovery, seed execution, crash/hang signal capture, coverage ingestion, and artifact reporting.","design":"Separate adapter responsibilities from aggregation/reporting so tests can use a deterministic fake adapter while production uses a real harness-backed adapter.","acceptance_criteria":"- Adapter interface is explicit.
- Crash/hang/coverage semantics are defined.
- Artifact/report expectations are documented.","notes":"Make sure this design can represent both local and remotely-produced fuzz artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:43.537420115Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.882288180Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","fuzz","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.882241142Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:43.825674499Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.7.2","title":"Implement harness-backed fuzz execution and truthful gate reporting","description":"Implement the real fuzz gate runner so it executes targets, collects actual crash/hang/coverage results, and emits truthful gate verdicts.","design":"Replace the current synthetic path entirely in the production implementation. The runner should aggregate adapter output, preserve reproducers, and distinguish infrastructure failure from target failure.","acceptance_criteria":"- Production runner executes real target/harness logic.
- Reports include truthful crash/hang/coverage data.
- Synthetic string-trigger behavior is removed from the live implementation.","notes":"A clear error taxonomy will matter here; do not collapse harness failures and target bugs into one generic verdict.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:44.172019035Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:47.218334532Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","implementation","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:44.447769140Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:47.218273058Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.7.3","title":"Retain deterministic fuzz fixtures while removing simulation from the live path","description":"Preserve deterministic test coverage by introducing an explicit test adapter and by updating test suites so simulation remains allowed only where clearly scoped to tests.","design":"Move current deterministic behavior behind a test-only adapter or fixture layer. Tests should assert both the real runner contract and the test-adapter fallback semantics.","acceptance_criteria":"- Deterministic tests remain easy to write.
- Production code path no longer depends on test simulation behavior.
- Unit/integration and gate-level coverage prove the separation.
- Detailed runner logs and fixture markers show when the real runner vs the test adapter was used.","notes":"The goal is to keep developer ergonomics while making the production truthfulness boundary obvious.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:44.813137384Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:24.354130160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","fuzz","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:45.084798409Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:20:47.487408938Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.8","title":"Implement real rollback-on-drop semantics for obligation guards","description":"Implement real rollback-on-drop semantics for obligation guards. The audit found that `connector/obligation_tracker.rs` explicitly states that in a real implementation the guard drop path would call `tracker.rollback()`, but today it only logs.","design":"Refactor the guard/tracker ownership model so unresolved drop can safely trigger or enqueue rollback without violating borrowing rules. Keep leak-oracle behavior as a secondary audit mechanism, not the only cleanup path.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unresolved guard drop performs real cleanup semantics.
- Logging-only fallback is removed from the production path.
- Leak-oracle/tests assert the new behavior.","notes":"The clean design matters more than preserving the current struct shape. Choose ownership primitives that make the correct behavior straightforward.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:22.769102546Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:40:57.927081015Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["obligations","placeholder-remediation","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:23.009539716Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.8.1","title":"Redesign obligation guard/tracker ownership for real rollback-on-drop","description":"Redesign the relationship between obligation guards and the tracker so drop-time rollback is possible and race-free.","design":"Evaluate the ownership model deliberately: direct mutable access, shared interior mutability, deferred rollback queue, or another pattern that preserves safety and determinism.","acceptance_criteria":"- Chosen ownership model permits real rollback-on-drop.
- Safety/determinism implications are explicit.
- Follow-on implementation scope is clear.","notes":"Avoid papering over the issue with more logging. This bead exists to make the correct cleanup path structurally possible.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:23.317563995Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:30.936609908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","obligations","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:30.936529418Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:23.562820065Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.8.2","title":"Implement rollback-on-drop cleanup path and lifecycle instrumentation","description":"Implement the actual rollback-on-drop path and the core lifecycle instrumentation needed to observe cleanup decisions. Validation breadth, leak-oracle ratchets, and regression coverage now live in the dedicated follow-on bead.","design":"Wire the new ownership model into guard drop, tracker state, and emitted diagnostics. Focus this bead on making the cleanup path real and observable; exhaustive proof belongs in the branch's validation bead.","acceptance_criteria":"- Drop triggers real rollback semantics.
- Core diagnostics are emitted for cleanup-triggering vs no-op drop paths.
- Production code no longer contains the logging-only placeholder behavior.","notes":"This branch should leave no ambiguity about whether cleanup is best-effort or required.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:23.870420905Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:21.321790758Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","obligations","placeholder-remediation","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:24.111932337Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:31.886784654Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2fqyv.8.3","title":"Add rollback-on-drop regression tests and structured cleanup diagnostics","description":"Add the regression-proof validation layer for rollback-on-drop: focused unit tests, integration/leak-oracle coverage, and structured diagnostics that explain when cleanup was triggered, skipped, retried, or failed.","design":"Separate behavioral proof from implementation. Use small unit tests for guard/tracker edge cases, integration tests for lifecycle cleanup, and operator-facing diagnostics that make rollback behavior inspectable without reading code. Logging should distinguish resolved drops, unresolved cleanup-triggering drops, and exceptional rollback failures.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit tests cover resolved, unresolved, repeated, and failure-path drops.
- Integration/leak-oracle coverage proves real cleanup occurs and regressions are caught.
- Structured diagnostics make cleanup behavior observable in logs or emitted artifacts.
- The validation surface is strong enough to prevent drift back to logging-only semantics.","notes":"The implementation bead can change internals freely; this bead locks in externally visible truth and debuggability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.782860942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:19.483403138Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.782860942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:30.782860942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.9","title":"Populate ecosystem health export from real telemetry inputs","description":"Populate ecosystem health export fields from real telemetry inputs instead of placeholder values. The audit found that `supply_chain/ecosystem_telemetry.rs` hard-codes `compromise_reduction_factor = 1.0` and always emits an empty `certification_distribution` while commenting that live data should populate them.","design":"Treat these fields as contract-level metrics. Define their provenance and formulas clearly, then compute them from authoritative telemetry inputs so reports stop claiming values that were never measured.","acceptance_criteria":"- Placeholder export fields are replaced with real computed values.
- Metric provenance and formulas are documented.

 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.9.2","title":"Implement ecosystem health metric computation and explicit missing-data semantics","description":"Implement the real metric computations and explicit missing-data behavior for ecosystem health export fields. Emit the core metadata needed for the later validation bead to assert provenance.","acceptance_criteria":"- Exported metrics are computed from real inputs.
- Placeholder constants/empty maps are gone from the live path.
- Missing or stale inputs produce explicit semantics rather than silent fake values.","notes":"Make sure downstream consumers can tell whether they are seeing measured values or explicit unavailability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:25.544893865Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:21.698119035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","placeholder-remediation","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:25.785643758Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:22:32.116162056Z","created_by":"CalmBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fqyv.9.3","title":"Add telemetry contract tests, fixture matrices, and metric provenance logging","description":"Add the validation and observability layer for ecosystem telemetry so computed metrics are provable, diagnosable, and contract-stable. This includes unit tests, report-level fixtures, and detailed provenance logging.","design":"Build a fixture matrix covering complete inputs, partial inputs, stale inputs, and missing inputs. Tests should assert both numeric outputs and explicit unavailability states. Logging/report metadata should record the upstream sources, timestamps, and fallback reasons that produced each metric.","acceptance_criteria":"- Unit and contract tests cover complete, partial, stale, and missing-input scenarios.
- Report fixtures assert both metric values and explicit unavailability semantics.
- Provenance logging or metadata explains how each exported metric was computed.
- Gates/docs/artifacts reference the new telemetry validation workflow.","notes":"Without provenance and fixture coverage, these fields will drift back toward unverifiable placeholders.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.165613328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:39:19.855005728Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.165613328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2fqyv.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:37:31.165613328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fuk0","title":"Fail closed on unknown offline coverage SLO metrics","description":"Why this exists: crates/franken-node/src/connector/offline_coverage.rs::check_slos() currently matches only coverage, availability, and repair_debt, but silently skips any other metric_name. That means a typo or unsupported SLO target is treated as if no alert were needed, which is a fail-open configuration bug in the dashboard/alerting path. What this task must do: reject unknown SLO metric names explicitly, add focused tests proving misconfigured targets no longer pass silently, and keep cargo-heavy validation on rch only if needed. Acceptance criteria: 1. Unknown SLO metric names return an error instead of being ignored. 2. Supported metrics preserve current behavior. 3. Focused tests cover the fail-closed path and a valid metric path remains green.","notes":"Patch landed in crates/franken-node/src/connector/offline_coverage.rs and a follow-on module-wiring fix landed in crates/franken-node/src/connector/mod.rs. Fresh-eyes fix still stands: check_slos() fails closed on unsupported SLO metric names by returning CoverageError::UnknownMetric instead of silently skipping misconfigured targets; existing focused tests now actually execute because offline_coverage is compiled under cfg(test) via connector/mod.rs. Validation status: rch cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib passed on jain; rch targeted tests connector::offline_coverage::tests::unknown_slo_metric_rejected and connector::offline_coverage::tests::slo_no_breach_when_healthy both passed on vmi1293453 after the module-gating fix. git diff --check on the touched file is clean. rustfmt --check surfaced unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other connector files, so formatting is not clean repo-wide but not due to this patch. Scoped rch clippy is/was run as a corroborating follow-up.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BronzeCave","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:51:18.023293117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:46:35.324403625Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T14:46:35.324375543Z","close_reason":"Implemented fail-closed unknown metric handling and enabled real test coverage via cfg(test) module wiring","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector","fail-closed","offline-coverage","slo-validation"]}
